messages.content: Summarize the following incident report. At 09:12 UTC the payments service began returning elevated 500s. On-call was paged at 09:15. Initial investigation pointed to a connection pool exhaustion in the primary database caused by a slow migration query deployed at 08:55. The migration was rolled back at 09:31 and error rates recovered by 09:40. Total customer impact was 28 minutes of degraded checkout, affecting an estimated 4200 sessions. Action items: add a query timeout to the migration runner, alert on connection pool saturation at 80%, and require migrations to run against a replica first. Please produce a 3-sentence executive summary and a bulleted list of action items.

note: negative case - model not on the search/free-tier allowlist, expect model_not_allowed for those keys
negative case - nonexistent model, expect a clear 4xx from the gateway
multi-turn conversation - full history counts toward prompt tokens

messages.content: Connection pooling reuses database connections instead of opening a new one per request.
What happens when the pool is exhausted?

note: should-not-cache example - time-sensitive question; think about why caching this is wrong

messages.content: What is the current status of the payments service?
